fault_hypothesis: On line 66, `int(x)` truncates (floors) each planned value instead of using a largest-remainder rounding method, so the sum of allocations does not equal the intended total when weights don't divide evenly.

constraints.intent: After building integer allocations via int(), their sum must equal round(sum(planned)) — violated when truncation loses remainders.

spec.expr: sum(allocations__1) >= sum(int(x) for x in planned__1) and sum(allocations__1) <= sum(int(x) for x in planned__1) + len(planned__1)

constraints.intent: The integer allocations must lie between the floor-sum and floor-sum plus number of recipients, and actually equal the target total.
At the end of the integer-allocation branch, the total allocated must match the rounded planned total.
The returned allocations must sum to the intended total, catching any loss due to truncation.
